title: The Vanishing Curator
description: Late at night, the city museum got an emergency call. A very valuable golden item called the Eye of Horus has been stolen from the east wing. Security cameras show someone desabling the ALARM and entering the VAULT room at exactly midnight. A GUARD log also confirms that someone entered without permission. Some people said they saw a red LASER tripwire being cut near the display. The suspect then ESCAPE through the back corridor. Your team has been called to figure out the hidden message left behind and catch the thief before they leave the city.

levels.title: Wire Match
levels.instruction: Connect each color on the left to its match on the right.

levels.title: Interrogation Room
levels.instruction: Answer all 5 questions correctly to get the code.

levels.title: Memory Grid
levels.instruction: Memorize the highlighted blocks then tap them in order.

levels.title: Crack the Lock
levels.instruction: Enter the level numbers where you found each code in order.

clues.text: The curator's badge was found near the VAULT room.
Security footage shows someone in an ALARM-disabling uniform.
A GUARD log shows an unauthorized entry at midnight.

questions.question: Where was the Eye of Horus stolen from?
What system did the suspect disable to enter?
What time did the unauthorized entry happen?
What was the code you found in Level 1?
Which color was NOT present in Level 2?

story_reveal: The curator staged the theft for insurance money. The passphrase VAGLE was his own initials — Victor A. Greene, Larceny Expert. The Eye of Horus was never truly lost. It was hidden in his private office all along. Case closed.
chapter_reveals: The hidden words have finally been decoded: VAULT, ALARM, GUARD, LASER, ESCAPE. These were not random clues, they were placed on purpose. Each word points to a step in the theft, almost like the thief wanted investigators to follow their path. It becomes clear that this was carefully planned, not a quick or careless crime. Someone wanted to be found, or at least wanted to leave behind a challenge.

As the investigation continues, the team discovers that the museum's security system was not simply turned off, it was skillfully altered. The wires were rerouted with extreme precision. There were no broken locks, no damaged panels, and no signs of struggle. This level of control shows that the thief had studied the museum's electrical systems and layout for weeks, maybe even months. This was the work of someone patient, skilled, and very familiar with the building.

When the suspect is questioned, their answers begin to reveal too much. They seem to know details that only an insider would know like the exact timing of the unauthorized entry at midnight, how the ALARM system works, and how to access the VAULT without raising suspicion. The evidence starts pointing in a clear direction: this was not an outsider. The thief is likely someone who works inside the museum.

As more clues are connected, the team uncovers a clear pattern. The thief didn't move randomly, they followed a specific route through the museum, avoiding cameras and security points in a way that only senior staff would understand. This pattern confirms the suspicion that the criminal had deep knowledge of internal operations. The list of suspects becomes smaller, and the pressure builds as the team gets closer to the truth.

Finally, everything comes together. The truth is shocking, the curator himself planned the entire theft. He staged it to claim insurance money. The strange passphrase “V.A.G.L.E.” turns out to be a hidden clue using his own name: Victor A. Greene, Larceny Expert. The so called stolen artifact, the Eye of Horus, was never actually taken out of the museum. It had been hidden in the curator's private office the whole time.

title: The Midnight Cipher
description: A secret society known only as SHADOW has been operating in the city library for months. They meet every night at MIDNIGHT in the old clock tower behind the reading room. Their symbol is a RAVEN holding a KEY — carved into the back of bookshelf seven. An anonymous tip revealed their exit CODE is hidden inside a copy of a mystery novel. The librarian found a note written in CIPHER left between the pages. Your team must decode their message before sunrise and expose the society. Every answer you need is hidden somewhere in this passage — read it carefully before proceeding.

levels.title: Find the Hidden Words
levels.instruction: Find all 6 words in order. First letters spell the code.

levels.title: Wire Match
levels.instruction: Connect each color on the left to its match on the right.

levels.title: Interrogation Room
levels.instruction: Answer all 5 questions correctly to get the code.

levels.title: Memory Grid
levels.instruction: Memorize the highlighted blocks then tap them in order.

levels.title: Crack the Lock
levels.instruction: Enter the level numbers where you found each code in order.

clues.text: The manifesto mentions a SHADOW organization.
They meet at MIDNIGHT in the old clock tower.
Their symbol is a RAVEN holding a KEY.
The exit CODE is hidden in plain sight.

questions.question: What is the name of the secret society?
Where does the society meet every night?
What was the code you found in Level 1?
Which color was NOT present in Level 2?

story_reveal: The society was a book club that enjoyed mystery novels. The SMRKCC passphrase stood for their meeting room: Sub-basement Meeting Room, Knitting Circle Corner. Mystery solved.
